trade.summary: Korea-Taiwan trade is extensive and centered on the semiconductor and electronics supply chain. The two are simultaneously the world's leading semiconductor memory (Samsung, SK Hynix — Korea) and logic chip (TSMC — Taiwan) producers — making them essential trading partners in the global chip ecosystem. Korea imports TSMC-made logic chips and exports memory chips, display panels, and chemicals to Taiwan. They are also fierce competitors in certain electronics segments.
top_products_a_to_b: memory semiconductors (Samsung, SK Hynix DRAM and NAND)
chemicals and materials for chip fab
disputes: Samsung and TSMC compete globally for chip market leadership
Samsung and TSMC competing for Apple chip orders creates commercial rivalry

diplomatic.summary: Korea-Taiwan relations are close economically but diplomatically constrained by Korea's formal One China policy. Korea does not have formal diplomatic relations with Taiwan but maintains robust unofficial ties through the Korean Mission in Taipei and the Taipei Mission in Korea. Both are US security partners (Taiwan via TRA, Korea via mutual defense treaty) and face authoritarian threats from neighbors. Taiwan's strategic importance and the semiconductor nexus make bilateral ties deeper than the formal diplomatic absence suggests.
recent_incidents: Chinese military exercises around Taiwan create shared anxiety in Seoul given proximity to Korean Peninsula scenarios

military.summary: No formal defense relationship (Korea follows One China policy). However, both countries depend on US security guarantees to deter authoritarian aggression from neighbors. Intelligence contacts reportedly exist at low levels. A Taiwan crisis would have immediate strategic implications for Korea — diverting US attention and potentially emboldening North Korea. Both militaries closely monitor Chinese military developments.
military.alliance_status: No treaty — parallel US-aligned security dependencies

financial.summary: Deep financial ties driven by the semiconductor industry. Samsung and SK Hynix are major customers of TSMC for certain chip types, and TSMC buys Korean-made specialty chemicals, gases, and display components. TSMC and Samsung compete for global foundry contracts. Taiwanese investment in Korea's semiconductor sector is growing. Korean institutional investors hold Taiwan semiconductor stocks.

energy.summary: Limited direct bilateral energy trade. Both Korea and Taiwan are major energy importers. Some petrochemical trade flows between the countries. Growing cooperation on offshore wind energy technology, where Taiwan has ambitions and Korean shipbuilders and engineering firms are potential suppliers.

scientific.summary: Exceptionally strong technology collaboration and competition in semiconductors. Korean and Taiwanese semiconductor firms are interconnected within the global chip supply chain. Joint research in advanced semiconductor manufacturing, display technology, and materials science is substantial. Both countries are global technology leaders and compete in standards bodies and research forums.
